summary: Frontend-only Playwright testing of the Shyara multipage conversion (react-router). 37/39 checks passed. Intro loader plays once per session and is skipped on revisit; hero, navbar, footer, deep links, scroll-to-top, mobile menu and all 4 routes work correctly. Single bug: hero-primary-cta is still an in-page anchor (#portfolio) instead of a router Link to /work as the problem statement requires.

ui_bugs.issue: hero-primary-cta ('View Our Website Portfolio') is rendered as <a href="#portfolio"> instead of <Link to="/work">. Clicking it stays on '/' and only updates the hash to #portfolio (no #portfolio anchor exists on Home, so nothing scrolls/navigates). The review request explicitly states this CTA must navigate to /work via client-side routing.

issues: Framer-motion console warning: 'Please ensure that the container has a non-static position, like relative/fixed/absolute to ensure scroll offset is calculated correctly.' Likely originates from ScrollTimeline.jsx useScroll target ref where the container lacks position:relative. Non-blocking but should be fixed by adding `position: relative` to the scroll container.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/components/landing/Hero.jsx, change the hero-primary-cta from `<a href="#portfolio" ...>` to `<Link to="/work" ...>` (import Link is already imported from react-router-dom).
Optional: Add `position: relative` (Tailwind `relative`) to the scroll-container in ScrollTimeline.jsx that backs the framer-motion useScroll target, to silence the scroll-offset warning.
critical_code_review_comments: Hero.jsx already imports `Link` from react-router-dom but still uses a plain <a href="#portfolio"> for the primary CTA — looks like the multipage conversion missed updating this one anchor.

IntroLoader.jsx writes sessionStorage 'shyara-intro-shown' inside onAnimationComplete of the outer wrapper. This works in practice but couples persistence to animation completion; safer to write the flag in a useEffect on mount so refresh/abort still hides it on subsequent visits.
All interactive elements expose data-testid attributes (good).
ScrollToTop in App.js correctly resets scroll on route change.
All external links (wa.me, mailto, tel) use proper rel="noopener noreferrer" with target=_blank on http(s) links.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multipage Shyara site at '/', '/services', '/work', '/process'. Intro loader (data-testid=intro-loader) plays only on first visit (sessionStorage key 'shyara-intro-shown'); clear sessionStorage before testing first-visit behavior. Verified: intro appears+disappears (~3s), nav routing + active red highlight (rgb(255,51,51)), nav-logo back to '/', intro SKIPPED on revisit, WhatsApp hrefs (wa.me/919584661610) with target=_blank, footer mailto/tel intact, direct URL deep links to all 3 pages, ScrollToTop on route change, no 390px horizontal overflow on all 4 routes, mobile menu open/route/close, process-step-1..4 present. ONE BUG: hero-primary-cta is `<a href="#portfolio">` not `<Link to="/work">`. After fix, just retest the two failing checks (Link.to=/work and click→/work).
rca_of_the_issue: Hero.jsx (line 81-88): primary CTA element is `<a href="#portfolio">` left over from the single-page version. During the multipage conversion this should have been replaced with `<Link to="/work" ...>` (Link is already imported on line 2). Reproduction: load `/`, click 'View Our Website Portfolio' → URL becomes `/#portfolio` and no scroll/route happens (no #portfolio anchor on Home page). Fix: replace the <a> with <Link to="/work" className=...> and remove the href attribute.
